title: Money arriving and staying
subtitle: A visualisation for practising the feeling of money that lands, settles, and remains.
excerpt: A short visualisation practice for Day 19 of the MONEY program. Use to build the felt experience of financial stability: money in the account, the account staying full, the familiar anxiety absent.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education as a companion visualisation for Day 19 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to practise the felt experience of stable wealth before it's fully real, or to counter the habit of imagining that every gain will be reversed.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for Day 19 of the MONEY program. The image is specific: money landing in your account, and staying there, and you staying easy while it does.
Sit comfortably. Close your eyes or soften your gaze. Three slow breaths.
Picture your bank account. Pick a number that currently feels like a lot — the number that would make you breathe out. See it there, on the screen. The digits, the decimal point.

Now notice your body's response to that number. The familiar pull to do something with it. The urge to move it somewhere, spend it, transfer it — to get rid of the feeling of holding it. Let that urge be there without acting on it.

Now, in the picture, stay on the number. Let it stay on the screen. One week passes. The number is still there. Two weeks. The number is still there. Nobody has come for it. Nothing has broken. You haven't spent it to escape the feeling of having it. It just sits there, steadily, doing its ordinary job of being available.
Notice your shoulders. Let them drop a little. The money is there. You are here. This is what stable feels like.
Open your eyes when ready. If a line came up that felt true or false, write it down.
Original to homemade.education as a companion visualisation for Day 19 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
